questions.question: What is the purpose of the nullish coalescing operator (??) in TypeScript?
options: A) It checks if a value is falsy (false, 0, '', null, undefined)
B) It returns the right-hand operand only when the left is null or undefined
C) It throws an error if the value is null
D) It converts null values to empty strings

questions.question: What does the optional chaining operator (?.) do?
options: B) Throws an error if the property doesn't exist
C) Safely accesses nested properties, returning undefined if any part of the chain is null/undefined
D) Assigns a default value to a property

questions.question: What does `for...of` iterate over?
options: A) The keys of an object
B) The indices of an array
C) The values of an iterable (arrays, strings, etc.)
D) The properties of a class

questions.question: What does `for...in` iterate over?
options: A) The values of an array
B) The enumerable property keys (including inherited ones) of an object
D) The types of an object's properties

questions.question: What does `Array.prototype.map` return?
options: A) The original array, modified in place
B) A new array with the results of calling the provided function on every element

questions.question: What does `reduce` do?
options: A) Removes elements from an array
B) Reduces an array to a single value by applying a function cumulatively
C) Splits an array into smaller arrays
D) Sorts the array in ascending order

questions.question: What does `find` return when no element matches?
What does `some` return?
options: C) true if at least one element passes the test, false otherwise
D) The count of matching elements

questions.question: What does `every` return?
options: A) true only if ALL elements pass the test
B) true if any element passes the test
C) An array of boolean values
D) The last element that passes the test

questions.question: What happens if no case matches in a switch statement and there is no default?
options: A) A runtime error is thrown
C) Nothing happens; execution continues after the switch block
D) It loops back to the first case

questions.question: Why is `break` important in a switch statement?
options: A) It is required syntax; the code won't compile without it
B) It prevents fall-through to subsequent cases
C) It exits the entire program
D) It resets the switch variable

questions.question: What is the key difference between `??` and `||`?
options: A) They are identical in behavior
B) `??` only triggers on null/undefined; `||` triggers on any falsy value (0, '', false, null, undefined)
C) `||` only works with booleans; `??` works with all types
D) `??` is deprecated in favor of `||`
